Basil.tagline: The fragrant king of herbs — essential in Italian and Thai cooking alike.
Basil.about: Basil has been cultivated for over 4,000 years and holds sacred significance in Hindu traditions. Tulsi (holy basil) is worshipped in millions of Indian homes. It is the cornerstone of Italian cuisine and features in Thai, Vietnamese, and Mediterranean cooking.
micronutrients: Vitamin K — 414µg (345% DV). Supports blood clotting and bone health.
Eugenol — primary essential oil with anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.
Beta-carotene — powerful antioxidant converting to Vitamin A.
benefits: Anti-inflammatory — eugenol inhibits the same enzyme as ibuprofen.
Antibacterial — essential oils shown to fight E. coli and Staphylococcus.
Stress relief — adaptogenic properties (especially Tulsi) help regulate cortisol.
Digestive health — traditionally used to relieve bloating and indigestion.
cautions: People on blood thinners should moderate intake due to very high Vitamin K content.
Basil essential oil supplements in high doses may be toxic — use only as food.
Basil.india_note: Grows well across India in warm months. Tulsi (Holy Basil) is grown year-round in most Indian households.
Basil.global_note: Warm-season herb. Italy, Egypt, and the USA are top commercial producers.
tips: Choose bunches with vibrant green leaves — no blackening or wilting.
Keep like flowers in a glass of water at room temperature — not in the fridge.
Refrigerating fresh basil turns leaves black within hours.
Grow your own for best flavour.
culinary_tips: Add fresh basil only at the end of cooking — heat destroys its delicate flavour.
Blend with olive oil, pine nuts, Parmesan, and garlic for classic pesto.
Tear leaves rather than cutting — knife bruising causes faster blackening.
growing_tips: Loves full sun and warm temperatures — dies below 10°C.
Pinch off flower heads as soon as they appear to extend leaf production by months.
Water at the base, not on leaves — wet leaves invite fungal disease.
Keep a pot on the kitchen windowsill for continuous fresh supply.
fun_facts: Basil is sacred in Hinduism — Tulsi is worshipped in millions of homes across India.
The word 'basil' comes from the Greek 'basileus' meaning 'king'.
Ancient Greeks believed basil had to be sown while shouting and cursing to grow.

Mint.tagline: The world's most recognised herb — refreshing, medicinal, and nearly impossible to kill.
Mint.about: Mint has been used medicinally for over 10,000 years. It features in cuisines from Indian pudina chutney to Middle Eastern tabbouleh to British mint sauce. There are over 25 species and hundreds of varieties globally.
micronutrients: Menthol — the active compound responsible for cooling sensation and digestive relief.
Vitamin A — 212µg per 100g. Supports eye health and immunity.
benefits: Digestive relief — menthol relaxes digestive tract muscles, reducing IBS and bloating.
Headache relief — topical peppermint oil shown to reduce tension headaches effectively.
Respiratory support — menthol acts as a natural decongestant.
Oral health — antibacterial properties fight bad breath.
cautions: Can worsen acid reflux — the relaxant effect may loosen the lower oesophageal sphincter.
Always grow in containers — mint is extremely invasive in garden beds.
Mint.india_note: Grows across India in cooler months. Available year-round in urban markets. Widely used as pudina in chutneys, raitas, and biryanis.
Mint.global_note: USA, India, and China are top producers for essential oil extraction.
tips: Choose perky, bright green bunches with no yellowing or sliminess.
Store upright in a glass of water in the fridge, loosely covered.
Fresh mint stays good for up to 2 weeks when stored this way.
Dry excess mint in the sun for 2–3 days and store for herbal teas.
culinary_tips: Add fresh leaves to water, lassi, or lemonade for instant refreshment.
Blend with coriander, green chilli, and lemon for classic pudina chutney.
Thinly slice and sprinkle on fruit salads as a finishing herb.
Add generously to biryani, pulao, and raita layers for fragrance.
growing_tips: Always grow in containers — mint is invasive and will crowd out everything else.
Thrives in partial shade — tolerates lower light than most herbs.
Water regularly — mint loves consistent moisture but not waterlogging.
Harvest up to one-third of the plant at a time to encourage regrowth.
fun_facts: Mint has been found in Egyptian tombs dating back to 1000 BCE.
There are over 25 species of true mints and hundreds of natural hybrids.
Ancient Romans wore mint crowns at feasts to stimulate appetite.

Aloe Vera.tagline: The immortal plant of healing — medicinal, low-maintenance, and nearly indestructible.
Aloe Vera.about: Aloe vera has been used medicinally for over 6,000 years. Ancient Egyptians called it the 'plant of immortality'. Today it is one of the world's most studied plants, used in skin care, traditional medicine, and food products. India has significant commercial cultivation in Rajasthan and Gujarat.
micronutrients: Acemannan — bioactive polysaccharide with immune-modulating and wound-healing properties.
Aloin — compound in the latex layer with strong laxative effects. Use sparingly.
Anthraquinones — antimicrobial and antiviral compounds found in the gel.
benefits: Skin healing — gel accelerates burn healing and reduces inflammation significantly.
Moisturising — widely used in skincare for hydration and reducing fine lines.
Digestive health — aloe juice may help with IBS, constipation, and acid reflux.
Blood sugar — some studies suggest aloe gel can help lower blood glucose.
cautions: Aloe latex (yellow layer under the skin) is a strong laxative — use in moderation.
Do not consume during pregnancy without medical advice.
Can interact with diabetes medications — monitor blood sugar if consuming aloe juice.
Aloe Vera.india_note: Grows year-round across tropical and semi-arid India. Rajasthan, Gujarat, and Andhra Pradesh have large commercial cultivation.
Aloe Vera.global_note: Grows year-round in tropical and subtropical regions. Available commercially as a shelf product globally.
tips: Choose plants with thick, plump, upright leaves — avoid soft or yellowing ones.
For consumption: use organic, food-grade products without aloin or artificial colouring.
Harvest the outermost leaves first — they are the most mature and gel-rich.
Store harvested gel in the refrigerator for up to 1 week, or freeze in ice cube trays.
culinary_tips: Remove outer skin and yellow aloin layer completely before consuming the clear gel.
Blend gel into smoothies — pairs well with lemon, ginger, and amla.
Mix with honey and lemon juice for a traditional digestive drink.
avoid_with: Never ingest the yellow latex layer under the skin
growing_tips: Plant in well-draining sandy or cactus soil — root rot from wet soil is the #1 killer.
Water deeply but infrequently — let soil dry out completely between waterings.
Tolerates neglect, shade, and drought — ideal first plant for beginners.
Repot when root-bound or when too many pups (offsets) form at the base.
fun_facts: Ancient Egyptians called aloe vera the 'plant of immortality'.
Cleopatra reportedly used aloe vera gel as part of her daily beauty routine.

Snake Plant.tagline: The ultimate air-purifying warrior — tough, low-light tolerant, and NASA-approved.
Snake Plant.about: Native to tropical West Africa, snake plant (also called mother-in-law's tongue) thrives on neglect. NASA studies rank it top for removing toxins like formaldehyde and benzene from indoor air. Popular in Indian homes for its upright, sword-like leaves and minimal care.
micronutrients: Saponins — mild soapy compounds with potential antifungal properties (not for consumption).
benefits: Air purification — removes 80%+ of indoor toxins per NASA research.
Low maintenance — survives in dim corners with rare watering.
Oxygen boost — releases oxygen at night, improving bedroom air.
cautions: Toxic if ingested — can cause nausea, vomiting in pets and children.
Avoid overwatering to prevent root rot.
Snake Plant.india_note: Thrives year-round in Indian homes; Madurai's warm climate is ideal indoors.
Snake Plant.global_note: Available worldwide as a houseplant; peaks in spring in temperate zones.
tips: Select stiff, upright leaves without brown tips.
Propagate easily from leaf cuttings in water or soil.
Wipe leaves monthly for dust removal.
culinary_tips: Not for eating; use in floral arrangements or as air freshener.
growing_tips: Use well-draining soil; water every 2-4 weeks when soil is bone-dry.
Thrives in low to bright indirect light — tolerates full shade.
fun_facts: Releases oxygen at night unlike most plants.
Used in African traditions for protection against evil.

Spider Plant.tagline: Prolific pup-producer and air cleanser — forgiving and fast-growing for beginners.
Spider Plant.about: Hailing from South African coastal regions, spider plants cascade gracefully with arching leaves and 'pups' (offsets) for easy propagation. They filter formaldehyde and xylene effectively. In India, they're a staple balcony plant in humid areas like Tamil Nadu.
benefits: Air purification — excels at removing indoor pollutants.
Easy propagation — pups root in water within weeks.
cautions: Brown tips from fluoride in water — use rainwater or filtered.
Spider Plant.india_note: Loves Madurai's humidity; grows vigorously in indirect light.
tips: Hang in baskets for trailing effect.
Snip and root pups for free plants.
Trim brown tips to maintain looks.
avoid_with: Direct sun, tap water high in fluoride
growing_tips: Keep soil lightly moist; allow top inch to dry.
Bright indirect light prevents legginess.

Peace Lily.tagline: Elegant drooper signaling thirst — superb air purifier for shady spots.
Peace Lily.about: Tropical understory plant from South America, peace lilies feature glossy leaves and white spathe flowers. They top NASA's clean air list for removing ammonia, benzene, and more. In India, they flourish in low-light homes and offices.
benefits: Humidity booster — great for dry indoor air.
Low light tolerance — thrives in bathrooms or north-facing rooms.
cautions: Toxic if eaten — causes mouth irritation, swelling.
Drooping leaves signal underwatering — quick recovery with water.
Peace Lily.india_note: Perfect for Madurai's indoor humidity; avoid direct AC vents.
Peace Lily.global_note: Widely available as potted plant; blooms repeatedly indoors.
tips: Buy with white blooms for instant elegance.
avoid_with: Direct sun, pets/children who chew plants
growing_tips: Water when leaves droop; use filtered water to avoid tip burn.
Low to medium indirect light.

Pothos.tagline: Indestructible trailing vine — air-purifying, forgiving, and endlessly versatile.
Pothos.about: Tropical vine from the South Pacific, pothos (devil's ivy) climbs or trails effortlessly. NASA-ranked for formaldehyde removal. In India, cuttings root in water glasses — a beginner's dream plant everywhere.
benefits: Easy propagation — roots in water within days.
Versatile display — hangs, climbs, or shelves perfectly.
cautions: Toxic to pets — calcium oxalate crystals cause oral irritation.
Pothos.india_note: Thrives in Madurai homes; roots easily from market cuttings.
Pothos.global_note: Ubiquitous houseplant; cuttings ship worldwide easily.
tips: Root stem cuttings in water — change weekly.
Train on moss poles for dramatic growth.
Trim leggy vines to encourage bushiness.
growing_tips: Water when top soil dries; tolerates dry spells.
Any light except deep shade.
fun_facts: Survives in dim fluorescent office light.

Monstera.tagline: Swiss cheese vine with dramatic fenestrations — trendy, fast-growing tropical statement plant.
Monstera.about: Rainforest climber from Mexico to Panama, famous for holey 'fenestrated' leaves and rare fruit tasting like pineapple-custard apple. Social media darling in India for its sculptural look. Grows huge — up to 20m in wild.
micronutrients: Proteolytic enzymes — aid fruit ripening and digestion.
benefits: Fruit nutrition — vitamin C, potassium rich when ripe.
cautions: Unripe fruit toxic — causes throat swelling ('Monstera mouth).
Monstera.india_note: Loves Madurai humidity; mist regularly for best growth.
Monstera.global_note: Trending houseplant; fruit rare outside tropics.
tips: Buy plants with aerial roots for authenticity.
Clean large leaves with damp cloth monthly.
culinary_tips: Harvest fruit only when hexagonal scales fall off naturally.
Rare indoors; buy from tropical markets.
growing_tips: Moist well-draining soil; water weekly.
Bright indirect light; rotate for even growth.
fun_facts: Leaves develop holes to withstand rainforest winds.
Fruit scales protect against premature picking.

ZZ Plant.tagline: Zombie plant survives nuclear winter — ultimate neglect-tolerant indoor warrior.
ZZ Plant.about: From drought-prone African savannas, ZZ (Zanzibar Gem) stores water in rhizomes for months-long survival. Glossy dark green leaves resist pests and dust. Indian offices love it for impossible corners.
benefits: Extreme tolerance — survives underwatering, low light, dry air.
cautions: Toxic if eaten — calcium oxalate raphides.
ZZ Plant.india_note: Ideal for Madurai AC rooms; survives summer heat waves indoors.
tips: Water every 3-4 weeks — less is more.
growing_tips: Cactus mix soil; bone-dry between waterings.
Any light level except full shade.
fun_facts: Named after botanist Zamioculcas — ZZ for short.

Fiddle Leaf Fig.tagline: Instagram-famous diva with violin-shaped leaves — dramatic but demanding.
Fiddle Leaf Fig.about: Tropical fig from western African forests, known for large, glossy, fiddle-shaped leaves. Interior designer favorite despite fussiness. In humid Indian cities, it grows tree-sized indoors.
benefits: Biophilic design — large leaves create jungle vibe.
cautions: Dramatic leaf drop from stress (light/water changes).
Sap irritates skin; toxic to pets.
Fiddle Leaf Fig.india_note: Madurai humidity perfect; avoid dry AC rooms.
tips: Wipe leaves monthly — dust blocks light.
growing_tips: Bright indirect light crucial — no deep shade.
Consistent moisture without soggy soil.
fun_facts: Leaves photosynthesize 10x more efficiently than average houseplants.

Rubber Plant.tagline: Glossy burgundy beauty — easier than fiddle leaf, equally dramatic.
Rubber Plant.about: Native to Indian Himalayas and Southeast Asia, rubber plants yield natural latex (not commercial rubber source). Bold, leathery leaves in green-to-burgundy shades. Easier-care alternative to fiddle leaf fig.
cautions: Milky sap irritates skin/eyes; toxic to pets.
Leaf drop from drafts or dry air.
Rubber Plant.india_note: Native to northeast India; thrives naturally in Madurai indoors.
tips: Peel leaf sheath before new growth for clean look.
growing_tips: Bright indirect light; water when top 2" dry.
Humidity-loving — mist or pebble tray.
fun_facts: Sap was experimented with for rubber (low yield).

Rosemary.tagline: Memory-boosting Mediterranean herb — aromatic, evergreen, and kitchen essential.
Rosemary.about: Evergreen shrub from sunny Mediterranean cliffs, rosemary's needle-like leaves perfume roasts and boost memory. Ancient Greeks wore it during exams. In India, it grows well in pots; popular in fusion cooking.
cautions: Epilepsy — may trigger seizures in susceptible.
Rosemary.india_note: Madurai winters perfect; protect from monsoon humidity.
tips: Harvest woody stems for BBQ skewers.
Dry sprigs whole — hang upside down.
culinary_tips: Skewers for grilled meats — infuse flavor.
Infuse olive oil with sprigs + garlic.
growing_tips: Full sun, gritty soil, minimal water — true Mediterranean.
Prune after flowering to prevent woodiness.
fun_facts: Latin name means 'dew of the sea'.

Lavender.tagline: Calming floral sedative — sleep aid, insect repellent, and perfume classic.
Lavender.about: Fragrant shrub from Mediterranean drylands, lavender soothes nerves and repels moths. English variety most medicinal. In India, grows in Himalayan foothills and pots; used in chai and potpourri.
benefits: Sleep aid — reduces insomnia, improves sleep quality.
Antimicrobial — kills skin bacteria, aids wound healing.
cautions: Hormone-like effects — monitor with endocrine issues.
Lavender.india_note: Cool Madurai winters ideal; hot summers challenge pots.
tips: Harvest buds just before full bloom.
culinary_tips: Pair with lemon, honey in cookies.
growing_tips: Full sun, alkaline gritty soil, excellent drainage essential.
Prune 1/3 after bloom.
No fertilizer — too rich = no flowers.
fun_facts: Victorians sent pressed lavender as love symbol.

Tomato.tagline: Lycopene superfood — heart-protective, versatile kitchen king.
Tomato.about: Andean native domesticated by Aztecs, tomatoes exploded globally post-Columbus. Lycopene antioxidant linked to cancer prevention. India grows 20M+ tons yearly — every region has local varieties.
cautions: Nightshade sensitivity — joint pain in rare cases.
Acid reflux — eat ripe, cooked tomatoes.
Tomato.india_note: Madurai rabi season perfect; hybrids yield 5kg/plant.
Tomato.global_note: Year-round greenhouse; peak summer field tomatoes.
culinary_tips: Roast with garlic/olive oil — concentrates flavor.
growing_tips: Full sun 8+hrs, rich soil, consistent moisture.
Pinch suckers for bigger fruit.
Calcium spray prevents blossom end rot.
fun_facts: Botanically fruit, legally vegetable (US Supreme Court).

Chili Pepper.tagline: Capsaicin painkiller — metabolism booster, mood enhancer, and heat deliverer.
Chili Pepper.about: Mexican native spread by Columbus, chilies contain capsaicin for pain relief and endorphin rush. India grows 1.8M tons yearly — Andhra Pradesh chili capital. Heat levels from bell (0) to ghost pepper (2M Scoville).
micronutrients: Capsaicin — pain relief, metabolism boost, cancer cell killer.
Vitamin C — highest of any vegetable by weight.
benefits: Pain relief — topical capsaicin for arthritis, neuropathy.
cautions: Handle with gloves — capsaicin burns skin.
Chili Pepper.india_note: Madurai climate ideal; Byadgi variety famous for color, mild heat.
tips: Succession plant every 2 weeks for continuous harvest.
Pick green for heat, red for sweetness.
culinary_tips: Toast whole dried for deeper flavor.
growing_tips: Full sun, warm soil; transplant after frost danger.
Pinch tops for bushier plants.
Harvest regularly stimulates production.
fun_facts: Capsaicin doesn't dissolve in water — explains milk's effectiveness.

Coriander.tagline: Dual-purpose wonder — fresh leaves AND seeds, digestion superstar.
Coriander.about: Ancient herb mentioned in Egyptian tombs and Sanskrit texts. Every part edible — leaves (cilantro), seeds, roots. India consumes 60% world production; essential in every kitchen.
benefits: Digestion supreme — relieves IBS, gas, bloating instantly.
cautions: Allergy rare — avoid if sensitive to fennel/anise.
Seeds heat blood — avoid excess in Pitta.
Coriander.india_note: Madurai rabi crop; succession sow every 2 weeks.
tips: Sow successionally — bolts quickly in heat.
culinary_tips: Roots best in curries, Thai green curry paste.
Leaves last longer stemmed in water.
growing_tips: Partial shade hot areas, loose soil.
Thin seedlings — overcrowding bolts.
Harvest outer leaves for continuous crop.

Sunflower.tagline: Happy face heliotrope — seeds for eating, petals for salads, oil powerhouse.
Sunflower.about: Native American domesticate, sunflowers follow sun (heliotropism) until flowering. Ukraine/India top producers. Seeds yield healthy oil; petals edible. Classic kid's first garden plant.
cautions: Birds steal seeds — net if needed.
Sunflower.india_note: Madurai rabi crop; hybrids yield 2kg/plant seeds.
Sunflower.global_note: Warm season worldwide; massive prairie fields.
tips: Sow direct after frost; thin to 12" spacing.
Harvest heads when backs yellow, seeds firm.
culinary_tips: Oil high smoke point — perfect frying.
growing_tips: Full sun, rich soil, consistent moisture till flowering.
Stake tall varieties against wind.

Marigold.tagline: Nematode-fighting companion plant — edible petals, sacred in India.
Marigold.about: Aztec native, marigolds (gainda) essential for Indian festivals, weddings. Roots exude alpha-terthienyl killing nematodes/rootworms. Petals color rice, substitute saffron. Every Indian garden staple.
micronutrients: Lutein + zeaxanthin — eye health, macular degeneration prevention.
cautions: French marigolds more medicinal than African.
Marigold.india_note: Madurai temple flower; Pusa Arka varieties disease-resistant.
tips: Harvest petals morning after dew dries.
culinary_tips: Petals rice coloring — cheaper than saffron.
growing_tips: Full sun, moderate water, any soil.
Self-seeds vigorously.
Interplant with veggies for pest control.
fun_facts: Hindu Day of Dead equivalent — guides spirits.

Fern.tagline: Victorian parlor prehistoric — humidity-loving, air-purifying shade dweller.
Fern.about: Ancient lineage predating flowers, ferns thrive in humid shade. Boston fern tops NASA clean air list. Indian species like Nephrolepis in bathrooms; classic hanging basket plant.
Fern.india_note: Madurai humidity perfect; bathroom/ kitchen ideal.
growing_tips: Moist soil always; peat-based mix.
Indirect light, high humidity essential.

Cactus.tagline: Desert survivor — water-storing spines, some pads/fruit edible.
Cactus.about: New World family with 2000+ species. Nopales (pads) and prickly pear fruit (tuna) dietary staples in Mexico. Indian prickly pear naturalized; decorative indoors. Classic windowsill plant.
Cactus.india_note: Madurai drylands natural habitat; forage wild pads.
tips: Burn spines with torch before eating pads.
culinary_tips: Nopales salad — slice thin, boil 10min remove slime.
growing_tips: Cactus mix, full sun, water 2-3x/month.
Rot #1 killer — dry soil always.

Orchid.tagline: Exotic epiphyte diva — long-blooming, low-maintenance air plant.
Orchid.about: World's largest plant family (28,000+ species), Phalaenopsis moth orchids easiest for homes. Southeast Asian epiphytes grow on trees. India has 1300+ native species; Phals bloom 3-6 months.
cautions: Ice cube myth harms roots — use room temp water.
Orchid.india_note: Madurai humidity ideal; east window morning sun.
tips: Cut spike above 2nd node for rebloom.
growing_tips: Bark/orchid mix; water weekly, dry between.
Bright indirect, 50-70% humidity.

Curry Leaf.tagline: Indian kitchen tree — carb blocker, hair growth, aromatic essential.
Curry Leaf.about: South Asian native tree, every leaf used in tadka. Contains mahanimbine blocking starch absorption. Grows 15ft; semi-evergreen. Essential every Indian kitchen — fresh superior to dried.
Curry Leaf.india_note: Madurai native; backyards grow 10-year trees producing tons.
Curry Leaf.global_note: Tropical only; fresh leaves rare outside Asia.
tips: Freeze whole sprigs ice cube trays.
culinary_tips: Root curry Kerala style — medicinal powerhouse.
growing_tips: Full sun/partial shade, well-drained soil.
Water moderately; drought tolerant.
Fertilize NPK 10-10-10 monthly.

Jade Plant.tagline: Money plant succulent — feng shui prosperity symbol, nearly indestructible.
Jade Plant.about: South African succulent with tree-like growth, thick water-storing leaves. Chinese call 'money plant' for prosperity. India loves for balconies; lives 70+ years, blooms pink winter.
tips: Leaf propagation — lay on soil, mist.
growing_tips: Cactus mix; water 2-3 weeks dry soil.
Bright light essential.

Money Plant.tagline: Feng shui wealth vine — water-rooted cuttings, air-purifying trailer.
Money Plant.about: Same as Pothos — Indian 'money plant' for heart leaves symbolizing wealth. NASA clean air plant. Roots drinking glasses classic starter. Grows anywhere — offices, bedrooms, kitchens.
Money Plant.india_note: Madurai homes — every balcony has one.
growing_tips: Water when dry; any light.
Humidity loving — bathroom great.

Tulsi.tagline: Sacred adaptogen — stress shield, immunity booster, household deity plant.
Tulsi.about: Hindu sacred plant (Vrinda), every Indian home grows Tulsi. Adaptogen reduces stress hormones. 3000+ years Ayurveda use for respiratory, immunity. Two main types: green Rama, purple Krishna.
Tulsi.india_note: Madurai household essential; daily puja plant.
tips: Morning dew Tulsi most potent medicinally.
culinary_tips: Pesto substitute — nuts, cheese, olive oil.
growing_tips: Morning sun, afternoon shade; rich soil.
Pinch flowers prolong leaf production.
Holy water weekly.

Lemon Tree.tagline: Vitamin C powerhouse — alkalizing despite acidity, versatile fruit tree.
Lemon Tree.about: Indian subcontinent native hybridized from citron, wild orange. Vitamin C prevents scurvy (sailors). India produces 3M tons yearly. Dwarf varieties pot-perfect; fragrant blooms year-round.
Lemon Tree.india_note: Madurai backyards; Kagzi variety best juice.
tips: Zest before juicing — oils most potent.
culinary_tips: Hot water extraction max vitamin C.
Preserve whole salt jar — Middle East style.
growing_tips: Full sun, acidic soil pH 6-7.
Heavy feeder — citrus fertilizer monthly.
Protect <28°F.

Bird of Paradise.tagline: Crane-shaped floral fireworks — South African drama plant.
Bird of Paradise.about: Cape Dutch native named for crane/bird beak flowers. Iconic hotel lobby plant. Rhizomatous perennial; slow but flowers 3-5 years. India grows well coastal areas.
benefits: Long vase life — cut flowers last 2+ weeks.
Bird of Paradise.india_note: Madurai coastal humidity good; inland pots.
growing_tips: Full sun, rich soil, ample water summer.
Mulch heavily dry season.
fun_facts: Oscar de la Renta floral muse.

Bamboo.tagline: Grass-tree hybrid — fastest plant growth, versatile wonder material.
Bamboo.about: Giant grass (1300+ species), some grow 3ft/day. India bamboo capital — 136 species. Shoots edible spring; culms building/food/medicine. Clumping vs. running root types crucial.
benefits: Super fast renewable — harvests 35% more oxygen trees.
cautions: Raw shoots cyanide — boil 20min change water.
Bamboo.india_note: Madurai: Bambusa vulgaris common; NE India bamboo rice.
culinary_tips: Bamboo rice (NE India) — shoots glutinous rice.
growing_tips: Full sun/partial; moist rich soil.
Rhizome barrier running types.
Divide clumps every 5 years.

Thyme.tagline: Mediterranean mini-shrub — antimicrobial powerhouse, culinary workhorse.
Thyme.about: Creeping Mediterranean native used since Egyptian times. Thymol major antiseptic compound. Essential roast chicken, stuffing herb. India grows well pots; lemon thyme popular.
Thyme.india_note: Madurai dry winters perfect; balcony pot essential.
culinary_tips: Bouquet garni bundle with parsley, bay.
growing_tips: Full sun, gritty soil, minimal water.
Prune after bloom prevent woodiness.
Divide every 3 years.
fun_facts: Greek soldiers bathed in thyme-infused water courage.

Hibiscus.tagline: Hair growth tonic flower — cooling drink, endless summer bloomer.
Hibiscus.about: Asian native, national flower Malaysia/Hawaii. Flowers hair growth; calyces (Roselle type) cooling tea. India: temple offerings, cooling sherbet. Blooms never stop warm climates.
growing_tips: Full sun, rich soil, prune shape.
Fertilize bloom booster monthly.
fun_facts: Hibiscus sabdariffa = Jamaica sorrel Christmas drink.
Ancient China: hibiscus = delicate beauty symbol.

Jasmine.tagline: Night-blooming perfume queen — sacred, sleep aid, temple offering.
Jasmine.taste: Not edible flowers / Tea mild sweet
Jasmine.about: Indian native, Mogra/Jui essential puja flower. Jasmonic acid regulates plant stress. Night fragrance peaks 10pm-2am. India's $1B+ jasmine industry; Kannauj perfume capital.
benefits: Sleep aid — reduces anxiety, improves sleep quality.
Jasmine.india_note: Madurai: Mullai jasmine festival; daily market flower.
tips: Evening water; morning harvest max fragrance.
growing_tips: Partial shade, moist rich soil.
Prune after bloom.
Root suckers freely.
